â€) processes personal data in the context of its digital activities. For example, websites, mobile applications, connected TV applications, registration systems, digital marketing, online professional services and business resources, as well as engagement and information services that are each managed by the IOC alone or in cooperation with other members of the Olympic Movement. We refer to these activities as the â€œServicesâ€ in this Privacy Policy.Â

It also details how the IOC processes personal data that it receives from other organisations, or via cookies and similar technologies that are integrated into another organisationâ€™s digital properties for the benefit of the IOC.Â

Other organisations may launch and operate websites, newsletters, e-commerce platforms or other services that are (a) related to the Olympic Games, Youth Olympic Games or to the Olympic Movementâ€™s activities, or (b) feature Olympics-related content. Such sites and services are operated independently from the IOC and are not covered by this Privacy Policy. We therefore encourage you to read the terms of use and privacy policies that are applicable to those sites and services. If we collect or otherwise receive personal data through them, this will be indicated in the siteâ€™s or service ownerâ€™s privacy policies and/or terms of use.

heading_content_part: In order to provide you with the best Olympics experience, we have made changes to the account sign up and login process. If you open an account via services offered by the Paris 2024 Organising Committee for the Olympic and Paralympic Games (otherwise referred to as â€œParis 2024â€) you will be able to create and access your Olympic ID account using the same credentials. And, if you have an Olympic ID but would like to create and access your account on services offered by Paris 2024, you can do so using your Olympic ID credentials. As a result, both Paris 2024 and the IOC have access to, and are responsible for protecting, the personal data you provide for the purpose of identity and access management. You can contact the IOC through the

heading_content_part: We use automatic tracking mechanisms like cookies and similar technologies to collect certain data while you are browsing our Services, or when you visit the sites or services of other organisations that we work with. A cookie is a small file, stored on your browser or computer hard drive, which is used to collect data about your interactions with websites and other digital properties. For example, information about your device (browser type, operating system, language, unique device identifier), network connections (Internet Service Provider, IP address, time zone), browsing history including date and time and pages visited, as well as general location such as your city and country.Â Â

decoder_heading: 2. Information that is collected through cookies and similar technologies

You can also manage cookies by configuring the functionalities of your internet browser.ÂTo know more about our use of cookies on the Services, and how to manage your consent to cookies, please consult ourâ€¯Cookieâ€¯Policyâ€¯athttps://olympics.com/en/cookie-policy

Unless the Terms of Services state otherwise, the Services are intended for users who are of legal age for data protection purposes according to the laws of the territory in which they reside. You can learn more about our age requirements here:https://olympics.com/en/age-consent

decoder_heading: 3. Age limitation

heading_content_part: . If we become aware that a child below age has provided us with personal data, we will take steps to delete their personal data and close their account. If you become aware that your child has provided us with personal data without your consent, please contact us.

decoder_heading: 4. Data retention

heading_content_part: 4. Data retentionWe will retain your personal data only for the duration necessary for the purposes identified in this Privacy Policy or as required to meet our legal requirements.Â

decoder_heading: 4. Data retention
ident_heading: 4. Data retention
heading_content_part: Where your personal data has been collected through a section of the Olympics.com website or associated mobile application dedicated to a specific edition of the Games, and where necessary to achieve its long-term mission to promote Olympic Movement and preserve the legacy of the Games and maintain user engagement, the IOC may continue to process such personal data even after the relevant OCOG (such as the Tokyo Organising Committee of the Olympic and Paralympic Games as established by the Tokyo Metropolitan Government and the Japanese Olympic Committee) has stopped its own processing of the data. The IOC will continue to process your personal data in accordance with this Privacy Policy.

Contractual necessityContractual necessity applies where you (if you are of the required age to enter into a contract) accept our Terms of Service and justifies the following uses of personal data:ÂÂ a) Providing the Services and personalising your experience of the Services by customising certain features based on your interests.ÂÂ b) Letting you participate in any game, contest, sweepstake or other fan engagement opportunity which we make available through the Services.Â

ConsentWe also process certain personal data based on your consent, which you can withdraw at any time, for the following activities:Â Âa) Sending you marketing communications, including via digital means, where your consent is required.Âb) Placing cookies, both via our Services and certain third-party sites and services, where your consent is required.ÂIn these cases, you have the right to port your data and to withdraw your consent.Â

Legitimate interestsWe also rely on our legitimate interests, especially for our users who may not be of age to enter into a contract, to provide secure and efficient Services that are consistent with our Terms of Service, and so that our visitors, partners and fans can receive high quality Services and enjoy Olympic-related content corresponding to their interests. Legitimate interests constitutes a legal basis for the following activities:Â

decoder_heading: 3. Legitimate interests

heading_content_part: a) Making available through the Services publicly available information related to the Olympic Games, Youth Olympic Games, Olympians and other athletes, sports and news stories.Âb) Providing the Services and personalising your experience of the Services by customising certain features based on your interests.Âc) Managing registration and user accounts.Âd) Securing and developing the Services.Âe) Communicating with you in relation to the Services.Â

heading_content_part: f) Providing you with the goods, services, information or content that you have requested.Âg) Transferring, storing or processing your data outside Switzerland or the European Union, including to or within the United States of America and other countries.Âh) Sending you marketing communications, including via emails, in cases where you can lawfully receive such communicationsÂ

heading_content_part: i) Carrying out fan and audience analysis and offering you a personalised experience. This includes our analysis of your interaction with our Services (including the communications we send to you), as well as your interaction with third-party sites, services and advertising.Âj) Evaluating the performance of the Services as well as third-party sites and services.Â

decoder_heading: 3. Legitimate interests

heading_content_part: k) Sharing data with other organisations who may offer and/or provide to you their services, and with OCOGs who may use this data to better understand their fans, evaluate the performance of the Services, personalise their services and products and communications and, to the extent legally allowed, send you direct marketing, including marketing related to their products, services and partnersÂ

ident_heading: ANNEX 3: Activities involving the IOC and other organisations within the Olympic Movement
heading_content_part: To improve user experience, the IOC and Paris 2024 use common IT resources, in particular an identity and access management system. This system supports user account creation and management on the Services as well as on certain services offered by Paris 2024 via itswww.Paris2024.orgwebsite and other Paris 2024 digital platforms, including Club 2024, Terre de Jeux, and GÃ©nÃ©ration 2024 (the â€œParis 2024 Servicesâ€).

decoder_heading: ANNEX 3: Activities involving the IOC and other organisations within the Olympic Movement
ident_heading: ANNEX 3: Activities involving the IOC and other organisations within the Olympic Movement
heading_content_part: Where you have created an account on the Paris 2024 Services, you will be able to create and access an Olympic ID account using the same credentials. And the same will apply where you have an Olympic ID account and subsequently create an account on the Paris 2024 Services. As a result, both Paris 2024 and the IOC will have access to (and will share between them), and are responsible for protecting, the personal data that you provide for the purpose of identity access and account management, in particular to avoid any account duplication and/or to ensure accuracy of the personal data related to a user account.

decoder_heading: ANNEX 3: Activities involving the IOC and other organisations within the Olympic Movement
ident_heading: ANNEX 3: Activities involving the IOC and other organisations within the Olympic Movement
heading_content_part: The IOC and Paris 2024 are joint controllers with respect to the processing of data in connection with the account creation and management mentioned above. In its role as joint controller, the IOC is responsible for maintaining the common IT resources that are used for such account creation and management.The legal basis for the processing of personal data in connection with this activity is contractual necessity.
